title: asperula cypria
intro: Asperula cypria, also known as Cypriot wood sorrel or Cyprus wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its high content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as alleviating headaches, fever, and respiratory problems. Asperula cypria is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be found in herbal supplements and tinctures, allowing for various forms of consumption.
